subject: Python 3.9 support
truth: Stop using Python 3.9; Flask 3.2.0 requires Python 3.10+.
quote: Drop support for Python 3.9.

subject: __version__ attribute
truth: Stop accessing flask.__version__; it has been removed.
quote: removed in Flask 2.1, while remaining compatible with both in

subject: RequestContext merged into AppContext
old: RequestContext was a separate context pushed alongside AppContext
new: RequestContext is now a deprecated alias; AppContext is not reused when dispatching a request
truth: Stop relying on RequestContext being a separate context; it is now a deprecated alias of AppContext and an existing app context is not reused during request dispatch.
quote: RequestContext has merged with AppContext. RequestContext is now a deprecated alias. If an app context is already pushed, it is not reused when dispatching a request.

from_fact: requestcontext was a separate context pushed alongside appcontext
requestcontext is now a deprecated alias; appcontext is not reused when dispatching a request

subject: Flask request dispatch method signatures
old: Flask subclasses overriding dispatch methods used proxy objects and no AppContext parameter
new: Methods now take AppContext as first parameter; old signature shows deprecation warning
truth: If you subclass Flask and override request dispatch methods, update signatures to accept AppContext as the first parameter; the old signature still works with a deprecation warning.
quote: Many Flask methods involved in request dispatch now take the current AppContext as the first parameter, instead of using the proxy objects. If subclasses were overriding these methods, the old signature is detected, shows a deprecation warning, and will continue to work during the deprecation period.

from_fact: flask subclasses overriding dispatch methods used proxy objects and no appcontext parameter
methods now take appcontext as first parameter; old signature shows deprecation warning

subject: Teardown callback error handling
old: If a teardown callback raised an error, remaining teardown callbacks were skipped
new: All teardown callbacks are called even if any raise an error
truth: All teardown callbacks now run even if one raises; previously an error would stop subsequent callbacks.
quote: All teardown callbacks are called, even if any raise an error.

from_fact: if a teardown callback raised an error, remaining teardown callbacks were skipped
all teardown callbacks are called even if any raise an error

subject: should_ignore_error
truth: Stop using should_ignore_error; it is deprecated. Handle errors in teardown handlers instead.
quote: The should_ignore_error is deprecated. Handle errors as needed in teardown handlers instead.

subject: redirect default status code
new: redirect() returns 303 by default
truth: Stop relying on redirect() returning 302; it now returns 303 by default, telling the client to always switch to GET.
quote: redirect returns a 303 status code by default instead of 302. This tells the client to always switch to GET, rather than only switching POST to GET.

subject: Python 3.8 support
truth: Stop using Python 3.8; Flask no longer supports it.
quote: Drop support for Python 3.8.

subject: SERVER_NAME behavior with host_matching/subdomain_matching
old: Setting SERVER_NAME restricted requests to only that domain
new: Setting SERVER_NAME no longer restricts requests to only that domain
truth: Setting SERVER_NAME no longer restricts requests to only that domain when host_matching=True or subdomain_matching=False is set.
quote: Fix how setting host_matching=True or subdomain_matching=False interacts with SERVER_NAME. Setting SERVER_NAME no longer restricts requests to only that domain.

from_fact: setting server_name restricted requests to only that domain
setting server_name no longer restricts requests to only that domain

subject: Flask.open_resource/open_instance_resource and Blueprint.open_resource encoding parameter
new: Takes an encoding parameter defaulting to utf-8
truth: Flask.open_resource, open_instance_resource, and Blueprint.open_resource now take an encoding parameter that defaults to utf-8 when opening in text mode.
quote: Flask.open_resource/open_instance_resource and Blueprint.open_resource take an encoding parameter to use when opening in text mode. It defaults to utf-8.

from_fact: takes an encoding parameter defaulting to utf-8

subject: Request.max_content_length per-request customization
new: Can be customized per-request
truth: Request.max_content_length can now be customized per-request instead of only through the MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH config.
quote: Request.max_content_length can be customized per-request instead of only through the MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH config.

subject: -e path precedence over .env/.flaskenv
old: -e path did not take precedence over default .env and .flaskenv files
new: -e path takes precedence over default .env and .flaskenv files
truth: The -e path option now takes precedence over default .env and .flaskenv files.
quote: -e path takes precedence over default .env and .flaskenv files.

from_fact: -e path did not take precedence over default .env and .flaskenv files
-e path takes precedence over default .env and .flaskenv files

subject: load_dotenv behavior with path
old: load_dotenv with a path did not also load default files
new: load_dotenv loads default files in addition to a path unless load_defaults=False
truth: load_dotenv now loads default .env and .flaskenv files in addition to a specified path, unless load_defaults=False is passed.
quote: load_dotenv loads default files in addition to a path unless load_defaults=False is passed.

from_fact: load_dotenv with a path did not also load default files
load_dotenv loads default files in addition to a path unless load_defaults=false

subject: Previously deprecated code
truth: All previously deprecated code has been removed; stop using any Flask APIs that were previously marked as deprecated.
quote: Remove previously deprecated code.

subject: Flask and Blueprint Sans-IO bases
old: Flask and Blueprint classes without Sans-IO bases
new: Flask and Blueprint classes have Sans-IO bases
truth: Flask (app) and Blueprint classes have been restructured to have Sans-IO bases, which may break code that directly inherits from or inspects these classes.
quote: Restructure the code such that the Flask (app) and Blueprint classes have Sans-IO bases.

from_fact: flask and blueprint classes without sans-io bases
flask and blueprint classes have sans-io bases

subject: Minimum dependency versions
old: Older versions of Werkzeug, ItsDangerous, Blinker
new: Werkzeug >= 3.1, ItsDangerous >= 2.2, Blinker >= 1.9
truth: Minimum dependency versions have been updated: Werkzeug >= 3.1, ItsDangerous >= 2.2, Blinker >= 1.9.
quote: Update minimum dependency versions to latest feature releases. Werkzeug >= 3.1, ItsDangerous >= 2.2, Blinker >= 1.9.

from_fact: older versions of werkzeug, itsdangerous, blinker

subject: Python 3.7 support
truth: Stop using Python 3.7; Flask requires Python 3.8 or later.
quote: Drop support for Python 3.7.

subject: _app_ctx_stack and _request_ctx_stack push/pop methods
truth: Stop using push() and pop() on _app_ctx_stack and _request_ctx_stack; they are removed.
quote: The push and pop methods of the deprecated _app_ctx_stack and _request_ctx_stack objects are removed.

subject: FLASK_ENV environment variable, ENV config key, app.env property
old: FLASK_ENV env var / ENV config / app.env
truth: Stop using FLASK_ENV, ENV config key, and app.env; they are removed.
quote: The FLASK_ENV environment variable, ENV config key, and app.env property are removed.

from_fact: flask_env env var / env config / app.env

subject: app properties: session_cookie_name, send_file_max_age_default, use_x_sendfile, propagate_exceptions, templates_auto_reload
truth: Stop using app.session_cookie_name, app.send_file_max_age_default, app.use_x_sendfile, app.propagate_exceptions, and app.templates_auto_reload; they are removed.
quote: The session_cookie_name, send_file_max_age_default, use_x_sendfile, propagate_exceptions, and templates_auto_reload properties on app are removed.

subject: JSON config keys: JSON_AS_ASCII, JSON_SORT_KEYS, JSONIFY_MIMETYPE, JSONIFY_PRETTYPRINT_REGULAR
old: JSON_AS_ASCII, JSON_SORT_KEYS, JSONIFY_MIMETYPE, JSONIFY_PRETTYPRINT_REGULAR config keys
truth: Stop using JSON_AS_ASCII, JSON_SORT_KEYS, JSONIFY_MIMETYPE, and JSONIFY_PRETTYPRINT_REGULAR config keys; they are removed.
quote: The JSON_AS_ASCII, JSON_SORT_KEYS, JSONIFY_MIMETYPE, and JSONIFY_PRETTYPRINT_REGULAR config keys are removed.

from_fact: json_as_ascii, json_sort_keys, jsonify_mimetype, jsonify_prettyprint_regular config keys

subject: app.before_first_request and bp.before_app_first_request decorators
truth: Stop using before_first_request and before_app_first_request decorators; they are removed.
quote: The app.before_first_request and bp.before_app_first_request decorators are removed.

subject: json_encoder/json_decoder on app/blueprint and JSONEncoder/JSONDecoder classes
truth: Stop using json_encoder/json_decoder attributes on app and blueprint, and flask.json.JSONEncoder/JSONDecoder classes; they are removed.
quote: json_encoder and json_decoder attributes on app and blueprint, and the corresponding json.JSONEncoder and JSONDecoder classes, are removed.

subject: flask.json.htmlsafe_dumps and htmlsafe_dump functions
truth: Stop using flask.json.htmlsafe_dumps and htmlsafe_dump; they are removed.
quote: The json.htmlsafe_dumps and htmlsafe_dump functions are removed.

subject: Calling setup methods on blueprints after registration
old: Calling setup methods on a blueprint after registration produced a warning
new: Calling setup methods on a blueprint after registration raises an error
truth: Calling setup methods on blueprints after registration now raises an error instead of a warning.
quote: Calling setup methods on blueprints after registration is an error instead of a warning.

from_fact: calling setup methods on a blueprint after registration produced a warning
calling setup methods on a blueprint after registration raises an error

subject: Importing escape and Markup from flask
old: from flask import escape / from flask import Markup
new: from markupsafe import escape / from markupsafe import Markup
truth: Stop importing escape and Markup from flask; import them from markupsafe instead.
quote: Importing escape and Markup from flask is deprecated. Import them directly from markupsafe instead.

from_fact: from flask import escape / from flask import markup
from markupsafe import escape / from markupsafe import markup

subject: SESSION_COOKIE_DOMAIN behavior
new: SESSION_COOKIE_DOMAIN does not fall back to SERVER_NAME; default is no domain (exact match)
truth: SESSION_COOKIE_DOMAIN no longer falls back to SERVER_NAME; the default is no domain, which browsers interpret as an exact match not a subdomain match.
quote: SESSION_COOKIE_DOMAIN does not fall back to SERVER_NAME. The default is not to set the domain, which modern browsers interpret as an exact match rather than a subdomain match.

from_fact: session_cookie_domain does not fall back to server_name; default is no domain (exact match)

subject: locked_cached_property decorator
truth: Stop using locked_cached_property; use a lock inside the decorated function if locking is needed.
quote: The locked_cached_property decorator is deprecated. Use a lock inside the decorated function if locking is needed.

subject: signals_available attribute
new: signals are always available; signals_available is deprecated
truth: Stop checking signals_available; signals are always available as blinker>=1.6.2 is required.
quote: Signals are always available. blinker>=1.6.2 is a required dependency. The signals_available attribute is deprecated.

from_fact: signals are always available; signals_available is deprecated

subject: Blueprint empty name
old: Creating a blueprint with an empty name was allowed
new: Creating a blueprint with an empty name raises ValueError
truth: Creating a blueprint with an empty name now raises a ValueError.
quote: If a blueprint is created with an empty name it raises a ValueError.

from_fact: creating a blueprint with an empty name was allowed
creating a blueprint with an empty name raises valueerror

subject: app proxy attributes for config keys
old: session_cookie_name, send_file_max_age_default, use_x_sendfile, propagate_exceptions, templates_auto_reload on app
new: relevant config keys
truth: Stop using app.session_cookie_name, app.send_file_max_age_default, app.use_x_sendfile, app.propagate_exceptions, and app.templates_auto_reload; use the corresponding config keys instead.
quote: Use the relevant config keys instead.

from_fact: session_cookie_name, send_file_max_age_default, use_x_sendfile, propagate_exceptions, templates_auto_reload on app

subject: Blueprint setup after registration
old: using Blueprint decorators and functions for setup after registering the blueprint worked silently
new: using Blueprint decorators and functions for setup after registering the blueprint shows a warning (will become error in next version)
truth: Using Blueprint decorators and functions intended for setup after registering the blueprint now shows a warning; in the next version this will become an error.
quote: Use Blueprint decorators and functions intended for setup after registering the blueprint will show a warning. In the next version, this will become an error just like the application setup methods.

from_fact: using blueprint decorators and functions for setup after registering the blueprint worked silently
using blueprint decorators and functions for setup after registering the blueprint shows a warning (will become error in next version)

subject: before_first_request
new: run setup code when creating the application instead
truth: Stop using before_first_request; run setup code when creating the application instead.
quote: Run setup code when creating

from_fact: run setup code when creating the application instead

subject: CLI commands on app.cli and blueprint.cli
old: required @with_appcontext decorator on custom CLI commands
new: no longer required to decorate with @with_appcontext, app context is already active
truth: Stop decorating custom CLI commands on app.cli or blueprint.cli with @with_appcontext; an app context is already active.
quote: It is no longer required to decorate custom CLI commands on

from_fact: required @with_appcontext decorator on custom cli commands
no longer required to decorate with @with_appcontext, app context is already active

subject: --eager-loading/--lazy-loading options from flask run
old: --eager-loading and --lazy-loading options on flask run command
truth: Stop using --eager-loading and --lazy-loading options on flask run; they have been removed. The app is always eager loaded first, then lazily loaded in the reloader.
quote: The app is always eager loaded the first

from_fact: --eager-loading and --lazy-loading options on flask run command

subject: Python 3.6 support
truth: Stop using Python 3.6; Flask requires Python 3.7+.
quote: Drop support for Python 3.6.

subject: config.from_json
new: config.from_file(name, load=json.load)
truth: Stop using config.from_json; use config.from_file(name, load=json.load) instead.
quote: config.from_json is replaced by config.from_file(name, load=json.load).

subject: encoding parameter on json functions
truth: Stop passing encoding parameter to json functions; it is no longer accepted.
quote: json functions no longer take an encoding parameter.

subject: safe_join
new: werkzeug.utils.safe_join
truth: Stop using flask.safe_join; use werkzeug.utils.safe_join instead.
quote: safe_join is removed, use werkzeug.utils.safe_join instead.

subject: total_seconds
new: timedelta.total_seconds
truth: Stop using total_seconds; use timedelta.total_seconds instead.
quote: total_seconds is removed, use timedelta.total_seconds instead.

subject: blueprint registration with duplicate names
old: registering the same blueprint with the same name silently worked
new: registering the same blueprint with the same name raises an error
truth: Stop registering the same blueprint with the same name; use name= to specify a unique name.
quote: The same blueprint cannot be registered with the same name. Use name= when registering to specify a unique name.

from_fact: registering the same blueprint with the same name silently worked
registering the same blueprint with the same name raises an error

subject: test client as_tuple parameter
new: response.request.environ
truth: Stop using as_tuple parameter on the test client; use response.request.environ instead.
quote: The test client's as_tuple parameter is removed. Use response.request.environ instead.

subject: CLI script_info passed to app factory
old: CLI passes script_info to app factory functions
truth: Stop relying on script_info being passed to app factory functions from the CLI; it is no longer passed.
quote: The CLI does not pass script_info to app factory functions.

from_fact: cli passes script_info to app factory functions

subject: blueprint name containing a dot
old: blueprint names with dots were allowed
new: blueprint names with dots now raise an error
truth: Blueprint names must not contain a dot; using a dot in a blueprint name now raises an error.
quote: Show an error when a blueprint name contains a dot.

from_fact: blueprint names with dots were allowed
blueprint names with dots now raise an error

subject: register_blueprint same name multiple times
old: registering the same blueprint with the same name multiple times was allowed
new: registering the same blueprint with the same name multiple times is deprecated
truth: Registering the same blueprint with the same name multiple times is deprecated; use the 'name' option to give each registration a unique name.
quote: Registering the same blueprint with the same name multiple times is deprecated.

from_fact: registering the same blueprint with the same name multiple times was allowed
registering the same blueprint with the same name multiple times is deprecated

subject: Python version support
truth: Stop using Python 2 or 3.5; Flask no longer supports them.
quote: Drop support for Python 2 and 3.5.

subject: JSON module
new: Flask's own JSON support (no longer uses simplejson)
truth: Stop relying on simplejson being used internally; override app.json_encoder and json_decoder to use a different JSON module.
quote: JSON support no longer uses simplejson.

from_fact: flask's own json support (no longer uses simplejson)

subject: flask run error handling on initial call
old: Errors on initial call were deferred
new: Errors on initial call cause immediate exit with traceback
truth: The flask run command now exits immediately with a traceback on errors during the initial call, rather than deferring them.
quote: present during the initial call will cause the server to exit with

from_fact: errors on initial call were deferred
errors on initial call cause immediate exit with traceback

subject: (response, headers) tuple behavior
new: Headers replace existing headers
truth: When returning a (response, headers) tuple from a view, headers now replace rather than extend existing headers on the response.
quote: headers replace rather than extend existing headers on the response.

subject: Error handlers for base HTTPException
old: Error handlers for base HTTPException received the original exception directly.
new: Error handlers for base HTTPException now receive an HTTPException instance; the original exception is available as e.original_exception.
truth: Error handlers for the base HTTPException now receive an HTTPException instance instead of the original exception; access the original via e.original_exception.
quote: outside debug mode, so error handlers can still access it.

from_fact: error handlers for base httpexception received the original exception directly.
error handlers for base httpexception now receive an httpexception instance; the original exception is available as e.original_exception.

subject: Python 3.4 support
truth: Flask no longer supports Python 3.4.
quote: Drop support for Python 3.4.

subject: Minimum Werkzeug version
new: Flask now requires Werkzeug >= 0.15.
truth: Minimum Werkzeug version is now >= 0.15.
quote: Bump minimum Werkzeug version to >= 0.15.

subject: Host header handling for unprintable Unicode characters
old: Unprintable Unicode characters in Host header resulted in HTTP 500
new: Unprintable Unicode characters in Host header result in HTTP 400
truth: Unprintable Unicode characters in the Host header now produce a 400 Bad Request instead of a 500 Internal Server Error.
quote: Using built-in RequestContext, unprintable Unicode characters in Host header will result in a HTTP 400 response and not HTTP 500 as previously.

from_fact: unprintable unicode characters in host header resulted in http 500
unprintable unicode characters in host header result in http 400

subject: URL matching timing
old: URL matching occurred when the request context was created
new: URL matching occurs after the request context is pushed
truth: URL matching now happens after the request context is pushed, not when it's created, so custom URL converters can access the app and request contexts.
quote: URL matching now occurs after the request context is pushed, rather than when it's created. This allows custom URL converters to access the app and request contexts

from_fact: url matching occurred when the request context was created
url matching occurs after the request context is pushed

subject: catch-all HTTPException error handler
old: A catch-all error handler registered for HTTPException would handle RoutingException
new: A catch-all error handler registered for HTTPException will NOT handle RoutingException
truth: A catch-all error handler for HTTPException no longer catches RoutingException, which is used internally during routing.
quote: A catch-all error handler registered for HTTPException will not handle RoutingException, which is used internally during routing. This fixes the unexpected behavior that had been introduced in 1.0.

from_fact: a catch-all error handler registered for httpexception would handle routingexception
a catch-all error handler registered for httpexception will not handle routingexception
associative: custom error handler for all exceptions

subject: lists returned from view functions
old: Lists returned from view functions were treated the same as tuples (interpreted as response data)
new: Lists returned from view functions are NOT treated as response data; only tuples are interpreted as response data
truth: Returning a list from a view function no longer works like returning a tuple; only tuples are interpreted as response data.
quote: Don't treat lists returned from view functions the same as tuples. Only tuples are interpreted as response data.

from_fact: lists returned from view functions were treated the same as tuples (interpreted as response data)
lists returned from view functions are not treated as response data; only tuples are interpreted as response data

subject: Python versions
truth: Stop using Python 2.6 and 3.3; they are no longer supported.
quote: Python 2.6 and 3.3 are no longer supported.

subject: Flask.make_response error type
new: TypeError
truth: Flask.make_response now raises TypeError instead of ValueError for bad response types.
quote: Flask.make_response raises TypeError instead of ValueError for bad response types.

subject: error handler caching
truth: Error handler caching has been removed; register handlers explicitly for each exception if you want to avoid traversing the MRO.
quote: Removed error handler caching because it caused unexpected results for some exception inheritance hierarchies. Register handlers explicitly for each exception if you want to avoid traversing the MRO.

subject: old deprecated code
truth: Old deprecated code has been removed.
quote: The following old deprecated code was removed.

subject: Blueprint view function names
old: Blueprint view function names could contain dots
new: Blueprint view function names may not contain dots
truth: Blueprint view function names may not contain dots; using a dot will cause an error.
quote: Blueprint view function names may not contain dots. :pr:`2450`

from_fact: blueprint view function names could contain dots
blueprint view function names may not contain dots

subject: Subdomain matching
old: Subdomain matching implicitly enabled by setting SERVER_NAME
new: Subdomain matching disabled by default; must pass subdomain_matching=True to Flask constructor
truth: Subdomain matching is disabled by default and setting SERVER_NAME no longer implicitly enables it; pass subdomain_matching=True to Flask constructor to enable.
quote: Subdomain matching is disabled by default and setting

from_fact: subdomain matching implicitly enabled by setting server_name
subdomain matching disabled by default; must pass subdomain_matching=true to flask constructor

subject: Blueprint url_prefix trailing slash
old: Blueprint url_prefix kept trailing slash as-is
new: A single trailing slash is stripped from blueprint url_prefix when registered
truth: A single trailing slash is stripped from the blueprint url_prefix when it is registered with the app.
quote: A single trailing slash is stripped from the blueprint

from_fact: blueprint url_prefix kept trailing slash as-is
a single trailing slash is stripped from blueprint url_prefix when registered

subject: Request.get_json
new: only accepts UTF-8, UTF-16, or UTF-32 (autodetected)
truth: Request.get_json no longer accepts arbitrary encodings; incoming JSON must be UTF-8, UTF-16, or UTF-32.
quote: Request.get_json no longer accepts arbitrary encodings. Incoming JSON should be encoded using UTF-8 per :rfc:`8259`, but Flask will autodetect UTF-8, -16, or -32.

from_fact: only accepts utf-8, utf-16, or utf-32 (autodetected)

subject: send_file
old: mimetype guessing and ETag generation for file-like objects
truth: send_file no longer guesses mimetype or generates ETags for file-like objects.
quote: Mimetype guessing and ETag generation for file-like objects in send_file has been removed.

from_fact: mimetype guessing and etag generation for file-like objects

subject: send_file
old: silently fell back to application/octet-stream when mimetype could not be guessed
new: fails loudly (raises an error) when mimetype cannot be guessed
truth: send_file now fails loudly instead of falling back to application/octet-stream when mimetype cannot be guessed.
quote: Mimetype guessing in send_file now fails loudly and doesn't fall back to application/octet-stream.

from_fact: silently fell back to application/octet-stream when mimetype could not be guessed
fails loudly (raises an error) when mimetype cannot be guessed

subject: template auto-reloading
old: Templates were automatically reloaded outside of debug mode
new: Templates are no longer automatically reloaded outside of debug mode
truth: Stop relying on automatic template reloading outside debug mode; set TEMPLATES_AUTO_RELOAD=True if you need it.
quote: Templates are no longer automatically reloaded outside of debug

from_fact: templates were automatically reloaded outside of debug mode
templates are no longer automatically reloaded outside of debug mode

subject: flask.run()
new: flask CLI (flask.cli module)
truth: Stop using flask.run() to start the local debug server; use the flask CLI via the click CLI system instead.
quote: local debug server through the click CLI system.

subject: error handler matching order
old: Error handlers for specific classes were not checked first
new: Error handlers that match specific classes are now checked first
truth: Error handlers matching specific exception classes are now checked before generic handlers, which may change which handler catches your exceptions.
quote: Error handlers that match specific classes are now checked first,

from_fact: error handlers for specific classes were not checked first
error handlers that match specific classes are now checked first

subject: Flask logging
old: Flask did not log by default when debug is disabled
new: Flask logs by default even if debug is disabled
truth: Stop assuming Flask is silent when debug is disabled; Flask now logs by default, configurable via LOGGER_HANDLER_POLICY.
quote: Flask will now log by default even if debug is disabled.

from_fact: flask did not log by default when debug is disabled
flask logs by default even if debug is disabled

subject: deprecated module functionality
truth: Stop using any previously deprecated module functionality as it has been removed.
quote: Removed deprecated module functionality.

subject: JSON response newline termination
old: JSON responses did not end with a newline
new: JSON responses are now terminated with a newline character
truth: JSON responses now end with a newline character, which may break clients that don't expect trailing newlines.
quote: JSON responses are now terminated with a newline character, because it is a convention that UNIX text files end with a newline and some clients don't deal well when this newline is missing. :pr:`1262`

from_fact: json responses did not end with a newline
json responses are now terminated with a newline character

subject: before_first_request handler invocation order
old: before_first_request handlers flipped the flag before invoking
new: before_first_request handlers flip the flag after invoking
truth: before_first_request handlers now flip the flag after invoking, which changes behavior for handlers that may fail or have side effects.
quote: Changed logic of before first request handlers to flip the flag after invoking. This will allow some uses that are potentially dangerous but should probably be permitted.

from_fact: before_first_request handlers flipped the flag before invoking
before_first_request handlers flip the flag after invoking

subject: session system
old: session system accepted byte strings for token verification data
new: session system now supports byte strings again (reverses a prior breaking change)
truth: The session system now accepts byte strings again; if you were working around a lack of byte string support, your workaround may break or be unnecessary.
quote: Added support for byte strings back to the session system. This broke compatibility with the common case of people putting binary data for token verification into the session.

from_fact: session system accepted byte strings for token verification data
session system now supports byte strings again (reverses a prior breaking change)

subject: default cookie serialization format
new: JSON
truth: Stop relying on pickle-based cookie serialization; cookies are now serialized as JSON by default.
quote: Changed default cookie serialization format from pickle to JSON to limit the impact an attacker can do if the secret key leaks.

subject: flask.session module alias
truth: Stop importing flask.session as an internal module alias; use flask.sessions instead.
quote: Removed deprecated internal flask.session module alias. Use flask.sessions instead to get the session module.

subject: flask.g storage context
new: stored on app context
truth: Stop relying on flask.g being scoped to the request context; it is now stored on the app context.
quote: flask.g is now stored on the app context instead of the request context.

subject: flask.Flask.request_globals_class
new: flask.Flask.app_ctx_globals_class
truth: Stop using Flask.request_globals_class; rename it to Flask.app_ctx_globals_class.
quote: flask.Flask.request_globals_class got renamed to flask.Flask.app_ctx_globals_class which is a better name to what it does since 0.10.

subject: custom JSON HTTP exception subclasses
truth: Stop relying on Flask's custom JSON HTTP exception subclasses; they have been removed.
quote: Removed custom JSON HTTP exception subclasses. If you were relying on them you can reintroduce them again yourself trivially.

subject: Python version requirements
new: Python 2.6 or 2.7
truth: Stop using Python versions older than 2.6; Flask now requires Python 2.6 or 2.7.
quote: Python requirements changed: requiring Python 2.6 or 2.7 now to prepare for Python 3.3 port.

subject: flask.request.json property
new: flask.request.get_json() method
truth: Stop using request.json as a property; call request.get_json() method instead.
quote: Added flask.request.get_json() as a replacement for the old flask.request.json property.

subject: Logger debug log setting
old: returned debug log setting even if not set explicitly
new: only returns the debug log setting if it was set explicitly
truth: Logger now only returns the debug log setting if it was not set explicitly, so code relying on a default debug value from the logger may now get None.
quote: Logger now only returns the debug log setting if it was not set explicitly.

from_fact: returned debug log setting even if not set explicitly
only returns the debug log setting if it was set explicitly

subject: Tuple return values from view functions
old: were arguments to the response object
new: have a defined meaning (status code, headers, etc.)
truth: Tuple return values from functions are no longer arguments to the response object, they now have a defined meaning (status code, headers).
quote: Changed the behavior of tuple return values from functions. They are no longer arguments to the response object, they now have a defined meaning.

from_fact: were arguments to the response object
have a defined meaning (status code, headers, etc.)

subject: Session storage timing
new: session is stored after callbacks
truth: Session is now stored after callbacks so that if the session payload is stored in the session you can still modify it in an after request callback.
quote: Session is now stored after callbacks so that if the session payload is stored in the session you can still modify it in an after request callback.

subject: Malformed JSON data handling
old: Malformed JSON data raised a ValueError resulting in a 500 Internal Server Error
new: Malformed JSON data now triggers a Bad Request HTTP exception (400)
truth: Malformed JSON data now raises a Bad Request HTTP exception instead of a ValueError, so code catching ValueError on JSON parsing will no longer catch these errors and the HTTP response code changes from 500 to 400.
quote: Malformed JSON data will now trigger a bad request HTTP exception instead of a value error which usually would result in a 500 internal server error if not handled. This is a backwards incompatible change.

from_fact: malformed json data raised a valueerror resulting in a 500 internal server error
malformed json data now triggers a bad request http exception (400)

subject: send_file
old: send_file accepts file objects for mimetype guessing and etag support
new: mimetype guessing and etag support from file objects is deprecated; pass filenames or provide mimetype and etags manually
truth: Stop relying on send_file to guess mimetype or generate etags from file objects; pass a filename string instead, or supply your own mimetype and etags.
quote: Mimetype guessing and etag support based on file objects is now

from_fact: send_file accepts file objects for mimetype guessing and etag support
mimetype guessing and etag support from file objects is deprecated; pass filenames or provide mimetype and etags manually

subject: static folder for modules
old: static folder name is auto-detected for modules
new: static folder name must be supplied explicitly
truth: You must now explicitly supply the static folder name when using modules; the old auto-detection is deprecated and will be removed in 1.0.
quote: Static file handling for modules now requires the name of the static folder to be supplied explicitly. The previous autodetection was not reliable and caused issues on Google's App Engine. Until 1.0 the old behavior will continue to work but issue dependency warnings.

from_fact: static folder name is auto-detected for modules
static folder name must be supplied explicitly

subject: after_request functions
new: called in reverse order of registration
truth: After request functions are now called in reverse order of registration.
quote: After request functions are now called in reverse order of registration.

from_fact: called in reverse order of registration

subject: OPTIONS method handling
new: OPTIONS is now automatically implemented by Flask unless the application explicitly adds 'OPTIONS' as method to the URL rule
truth: OPTIONS is now automatically implemented by Flask unless the application explicitly adds 'OPTIONS' as method to the URL rule.
quote: OPTIONS is now automatically implemented by Flask unless the application explicitly adds 'OPTIONS' as method to the URL rule. In this case no automatic OPTIONS handling kicks in.

from_fact: options is now automatically implemented by flask unless the application explicitly adds 'options' as method to the url rule

subject: context processors
old: context processors could override values passed directly to render function
new: context processors will no longer override values passed directly to the render function
truth: Context processors will no longer override values passed directly to the render function.
quote: Context processors will no longer override values passed directly to the render function.

from_fact: context processors could override values passed directly to render function
context processors will no longer override values passed directly to the render function

subject: autoescaping
old: autoescaping was active for all templates
new: autoescaping is only active for .html, .htm, .xml and .xhtml templates
truth: Autoescaping is no longer active for all templates; it is only active for .html, .htm, .xml and .xhtml.
quote: Autoescaping is no longer active for all templates. Instead it is only active for .html, .htm, .xml and .xhtml.

from_fact: autoescaping was active for all templates
autoescaping is only active for .html, .htm, .xml and .xhtml templates

subject: support for zipped applications
truth: Support for zipped applications has been dropped.
quote: (temporarily) dropped support for zipped applications. This was a rarely used feature and led to some confusing behavior.

subject: create_jinja_loader
truth: Support for create_jinja_loader has been removed.
quote: Removed support for create_jinja_loader which is no longer used in 0.5 due to the improved module support.

subject: Server default bind address
old: server listened on all interfaces (0.0.0.0) by default
new: server listens on 127.0.0.1 by default
truth: The development server now binds to 127.0.0.1 by default instead of all interfaces; external machines can no longer reach the server unless explicitly configured.
quote: Server listens on 127.0.0.1 by default now to fix issues with chrome.

from_fact: server listened on all interfaces (0.0.0.0) by default

subject: send_file parameters
truth: Stop using attachment_filename, cache_timeout, and add_etags parameters on send_file; use download_name, max_age, and etag instead.
quote: ``download_name`` replaces ``attachment_filename``, ``max_age``

subject: send_from_directory parameter
truth: Stop using filename parameter on send_from_directory; use path instead.
quote: ``AppContext`` as the first parameter, instead of using the proxy objects.

subject: RequestContext.g property
truth: Stop accessing g via RequestContext; use the g object directly on the app context instead.

new: store data on g with a unique prefix

new: Config.from_file

new: Raises ValueError

old: Error handlers for InternalServerError or 500 received the original unhandled exception directly as the argument.
new: Error handlers for InternalServerError or 500 always receive an InternalServerError instance; the original exception is available as e.original_exception.
